messages.content:
Supplier labels must contain first aid measures in case of an incident
a. True
b. False
c. Only if the hazardous material is a health hazard
b. False. Supplier labels must contain first aid measures regardless of the type of hazard of the material.

Who should have access to SDS information?
a. Workers
b. First aid attendants
c. Supervisors
d. All of the above.
d. All of the above.

Which information is required on an SDS in Canada?
a. Transportation Information
b. First Aid Measures
c. Ecological Information
d. A and b
b. First Aid Measures

What type of instructions should a worker receive at a worksite before handling a WHMIS-classified product?
a. The type of hazard associated with the product
b. The type of PPE required to handle the product if any is required
c. The location of the nearest first aid station (or eye wash station if required)
d. All of the above
d. All of the above.

: Which of these categories are NOT covered by WHMIS Regulation?
a. Cosmetics
b. Pesticides
c. Hazardous Waste
d. All of the above
a. Cosmetics.

Hazards associated with manual handling are related to:
a. The type of object that is being handled
b. The pace of work
c. The work environment
d. All of the above
d. All of the above.

A musculoskeletal injury can be caused by:
a. Using an awkward posture to lift an object
b. Repetitive tasks
c. Moving heavy objects for a full shift
d. All of the above
d. All of the above.

A good way to move an item that is too heavy to carry alone is to:
a. Ask a co-worker for assistance
b. Use a cart
c. Both of the above
d. None of the above
c. Both of the above.

Which of the following are considered safe lifting techniques
a. Test the weight of the item before grasping it
b. Place your feet shoulder width apart for stability
c. Use your back to power the lift
d. a and b
d. a and b

It is preferable to push rather than pull an object if both options are available
a. True
b. False
a. True

Only incidents in which an employee is physically harmed need to be reported:
a. True
b. False
b. False. All incidents, regardless of whether or not there was physical harm, must be reported.

Examples of Workplace Harassment include:
a. Excluding a worker from a team meeting for no valid reason
b. Making discriminatory statements about a co-worker’s nationality
c. Asking a team member to work overtime to complete a report before deadline
d. A. and b.
e. All of the abov
d. A. and b.

A threat of domestic violence can be reported to a supervisor if the aggressor threatens to harm the worker while they are at work:
a. True
b. False
a. True.

A supervisor is required to act when there is a suspected case of sexual harassment:
a. Only if it is reported in writing
b. Only if the worker or one of their co-workers makes a complaint
c. As soon as they become aware of the situation
c. As soon as they become aware of the situation.

An investigation includes interviews with:
a. The employee who has allegedly been harassed
b. The employee who is the alleged harasser
c. Any witnesses to the event(s) of harassment
d. All of the above
d. All of the above.
